exemplar.title:
To what extent has Amazon's strict management and surveillance of
employees affected its profitability?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent has Amazon's strict management and surveillance of
employees affected its profitability?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive context throughout the report.
The research question is clearly stated, well-focused and consistently guides the analysis.
Contextual details about Amazonâs operations and controversies frame the investigation effectively.
weaknesses: The link between âunion-bustingâ and the narrower focus on strict management and surveillance is not always clearly distinguished.
There is no dedicated methodology section explaining source selection or credibility checks.
Primary research is absent, and some source choices lack justification.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place topic communication and research question formulation solidly in the 5â6 band, while research methodology sits in the 3â4 band. Averaging these performances and weighting the clearer strengths more heavily yields a final Focus and Method score of 5 out of 6. This honours the clear framing and focused RQ while recognising the missing explicit methodology.

strengths: Business theories and concepts are applied clearly, e.g. Herzbergâs two-factor and Vroomâs expectancy models.
Subject knowledge is coherent, demonstrated by detailed background and operational insights.
Terminology such as âlabour turnoverâ and ânet profit marginâ is used accurately and consistently.
weaknesses: Several key statistics (e.g. 150% turnover rate) lack precise in-text citation to primary data.
Occasional conceptual slips (conflating union-busting with surveillance) limit full coherence.
Source citations are sometimes too vague (e.g. âbusiness textbookâ without specifics).
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand scores of 3 for application, 4 for subject knowledge, and 4 for terminology average to just under the upper band. The clear and coherent use of theories and terminology merits an upper-3â4 band score. A final score of 4 out of 6 reflects mostly strong knowledge and application, tempered by the occasional citation and conceptual lapses.

strengths: Research draws on a broad range of secondary sources clearly linked to the research question.
Analysis links motivation theories to financial outcomes with a coherent logical flow.
Argument is developed through distinct sections, culminating in a synthesized conclusion.
weaknesses: Analysis often rests on single-source claims with limited triangulation.
Quantitative linkages (e.g. dollar impacts of turnover) are thin or asserted rather than fully evidenced.
Evaluation of limitations and alternative viewpoints remains superficial.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Critical Thinking strands fall in the 4â6 band but cluster at the lower end (research quality ~5, analysis ~4, evaluation ~4). The student shows clear analysis and a coherent argument, but weaknesses in triangulation and depth of evaluation suggest a modest score of 4 out of 12. This downweighting preserves proportionality while honouring the reportâs genuine analytical strengths.

strengths: The reportâs overall structure meets required sections and logical order, with numbered subsections.
Consistent font, spacing, and labelling of figures enhance readability.
Table of contents is well-structured and facilitates navigation.
weaknesses: Referencing format is inconsistent (broken links, missing italics) and footnote style varies.
No abstract is provided, and a few tables run off page margins.
Subsection indentation and figure captions lack uniformity and clarity.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation strands are in the top band (3â4) but exhibit enough minor issuesâabsent abstract, inconsistent citations, formatting slipsâto justify a mid-band score of 3 out of 4. The structure and layout are generally effective, but faults prevent a perfect mark.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Strengths:** The research questionââTo what extent has Amazonâs strict management and surveillance of employees affected its profitability?ââis explicitly stated, narrowly ties the independent variable (management practices) to the dependent variable (profitability) and guides all major sections (motivation theories, turnover costs, net-profit analysis).  
**Limitations:** The phrase âto what extentâ is addressed largely qualitatively; quantitative criteria for measuring the extent are not fully designed. Even so, the RQ is sufficiently focused and consistently referenced in discussion, meriting the upper band. A score of 5 recognises minor focus gaps while rewarding clear formulation.

A coherent argument runs from motivation theories through financial outcomes and ends with a conclusion, showing development. Evaluation exists (e.g., weighing short-term vs long-term effects) but remains partially critical; alternative viewpoints and limitations are only briefly acknowledged. Thus it achieves the 4-6 band but not the upper tier.

The essay applies established business theories (Hofstede, Herzberg, Vroom, Self-Determination) accurately and links them to Amazonâs warehouse context, demonstrating clear and generally coherent knowledge. Occasional conceptual slips (e.g., conflating union-busting with surveillance) prevent a fully *comprehensive* treatment, but the knowledge is clearly beyond anecdotal, warranting the upper tier of the 3-4 band.

Terminology such as âlabour turnover,â ânet profit margin,â âinstrumentalityâ and âmaintenance factorsâ is used correctly and consistently. Minor spelling errors (âcultrualâ) and occasional lapses in precision (mixing âprofitâ and âprofitabilityâ) show the use is not flawless, yet it remains largely accurate, placing it at the top of the 3-4 band.

**Positive:** The student analyses how surveillance impacts each motivation theory and links these effects to productivity and cost variables.  
**Negative:** Quantitative linkage to profitability (e.g., calculating actual dollar impact of turnover) is thin, and some conclusions are asserted rather than evidenced. The analysis is clear but not fully *well-supported*, justifying a low-mid score (4) in the 4-6 band.

**Strengths:** Data such as turnover percentages, cost-of-replacement estimates, and net profit figures are inserted in the argument at appropriate points; diagrams are included.  
**Weaknesses:** Several statistics lack in-text quantitative analysis, and some citations are broken or duplicated. The material is *mostly* relevant but sometimes anecdotal or unverified. Hence the work sits at the lower reach of the 3-4 band (score 3).

**Evidence:** Roughly 35 secondary sources (news articles, NGO reports, company filings, academic papers) are cited, showing a reasonable spread.  
**Weaknesses:** There is no explicit methodology section explaining source selection, credibility checks, or data collection process; primary research is absent; some sources (blogs, Vice, Engadget) are of uneven academic rigour. The range is âgenerally relevantâ but evidence of *effective and informed* selection is only partial. This fits the 3-4 band, low end.

Research draws on multiple external data points (injury statistics, turnover cost studies, financial statements) that clearly relate to the RQ. However, some claims rely on single reports or news sources without triangulation, limiting excellence. Overall relevance is clear and breadth reasonable, fitting solidly in the 4-6 band, mid-score 5.

Consistent font, spacing, page numbers, and citation style appear, and images are labelled. Referencing format is sometimes inconsistent (broken links, missing italics) and a few tables run off margins, preventing flawless layout. Overall layout considerations are âpresent and correctly appliedâ in most instances, meriting 3 in the top band.

**Strengths:** The student states the topic (âAnalysing the Effects of Amazonâs Union-Bustingâ) and the business focus (impact of managerial surveillance on profitability) in the title page, introduction and throughout the analysis section. Clear contextual details about Amazonâs operations and recent controversies show that the topic is well framed.  
**Limitations:** Although mostly clear, the link between âunion-bustingâ and the narrower lens of âstrict management and surveillanceâ is not always distinguished, so a small element of ambiguity remains.  
On balance the topic is âcommunicated accurately and effectively with comprehensive explanation,â fitting the 5-6 band; a mid-band score of 5 is most representative.

Required EE sections (title page, contents, introduction, analysis, conclusion, bibliography) are present and logically ordered; subsections are numbered. Minor omissions such as an explicit abstract and occasional page misalignment prevent a perfect execution, so a score of 3 (within the 3-4 band) is appropriate.

annotations.comment: The table of contents is well-structured with clear numbering and hierarchy, facilitating navigation.
The introduction supplies solid background on Amazonâs evolution and recent controversies, demonstrating subject knowledge.
Footnote formatting is inconsistent and source details lack italics or parentheses; adopt a uniform citation style.
The title page communicates the investigation topic and business focus clearly, framing the context effectively.
The research question is succinct and focused, linking management surveillance directly to profitability outcomes.
The word count is provided at the start and remains within the 4000-word limit, meeting the formatting requirement.
Indentation of subsections in the contents is inconsistent; align levels uniformly to improve clarity.
The application of Hofstede dimensions effectively links cultural factors to demotivation in Amazon warehouses.
A URL is used as a section header, disrupting flow; replace with a descriptive title and move the link to footnotes or bibliography.
The cultural analysis section accurately identifies misalignment between surveillance and employee values.
Consider incorporating primary data or academic studies to triangulate employee sentiments and strengthen validity.
The discussion of Americaâs indulgence factor is insightful but repeats points from previous dimension analysis.
Reliance on a single employee survey without triangulation weakens the argumentâs research quality.
The 150% turnover rate statistic is presented without precise in-text citation to its primary data source.
The Career Choice program section demonstrates critical thinking by linking training benefits to motivation and retention.
The evaluation of program impact is largely qualitative; consider adding quantitative retention figures to support conclusions.
The descriptive accounts of surveillance vividly illustrate its impact on autonomy and relatedness.
The absence of a dedicated methodology section leaves source selection criteria and credibility checks unspecified.
Herzbergâs two-factor theory is applied clearly to explain maintenance versus motivational factors in Amazon warehouses.
Statistics on injury impacts (52% physical, 57% mental) lack discussion on source credibility and sampling methods.
The discussion of turnover effects on productivity shows solid understanding of operational impacts.
The figure lacks a clear caption specifying data sources and time frame; add a descriptive label beneath the chart.
The conclusion synthesizes motivation and financial findings well but reiterates analysis rather than offering evaluative judgment.
Consider adding a reflective comment on decisions and planning at the end to demonstrate personal engagement.
Bibliography entries vary in formatâsome include full details, others only URLs; standardize to one citation style.
The extensive bibliography indicates thorough secondary research, enhancing the investigationâs credibility.
Vroomâs expectancy equation is correctly stated and integrated, reinforcing conceptual understanding.
Citing the formula source as a âbusiness textbookâ is too vague; specify title, author, and edition for credibility.
The leadership quote from Jeff Bezos is well-chosen to illustrate a culture of fear.
The discussion remains one-sided; incorporate alternative viewpoints or limitations to deepen critical evaluation.
Paragraphs in the conclusion could begin with concise topic sentences summarizing key insights to improve readability.
The financial analysis clearly defines labour turnover and its cost implications in percentage terms.
Footnote callouts in the body disrupt reading flow; integrate superscripts consistently and move URLs to bibliography.
The net profit calculation table supports quantitative analysis but column headers for years appear reversed.
Including the net profit calculation table enhances analytical depth by showing actual percentage results.
The fourth-quarter net profit figure chart conveys trends effectively, reinforcing financial analysis.
Several popular media sources (blogs, vice) lack explicit evaluation of credibility; justify their inclusion.
Evaluate and annotate source credibility in a methodology or reflection section to strengthen critical evaluation.
Bibliography formatting on the final page has dangling dashes and line breaks; clean up entries for consistency.
Including DOIs for journal articles demonstrates attention to scholarly sourcing and supports verification.
